Debuggable , DebugListener , Derivable , Executable , Instantiable , ModelErrorHandler , MoMLExportable , Nameable , java.io.Serializable, TypedActor public class CommandListener extends Receiver This actor will output a true whenever a specified co

s actor will output a true whenever a specified command with the specified house and unit code is detected. Only commands that are present or absent are supported by this actor. Use LevelSensor for commands (like BRIGHT and DIM) that have l

for commands (like BRIGHT and DIM) that have levels associated with them. A typical use of this actor is to have a motion sensor (or "occupancy sensor") with the specified house and unit code. If there is a such a motion sensor, then this a

e. If there is a such a motion sensor, then this actor will output true when it detects command. Note that an X10 motion sensor can be used to turn on and off devices directly if they have the same house and unit codes. The motion sensor wil

ame house and unit codes. The motion sensor will broadcast an on command and after some time an off command for that house and unit code. This actor, however, only reacts to the on command by producing an output true . Note also that the CM

y producing an output true . Note also that the CM17A wireless interface is unidirectional, only sending commands to the X10 network, and not receiving from it. Thus, this actor will not work with it (or more precisely, it will only report

ith it (or more precisely, it will only report commands that are sent out from the same serial port). Since: Ptolemy II 4.0 Version: $Id: CommandListener.java,v 1.14 2004/04/13 03:10:27 cxh Exp $ Author: Colin Cochran, Edward A. Lee See Also: Leve
